glossary.definition: Navigation in the Earth-Moon system, including trajectories between Earth orbit, translunar transfer, lunar orbit, and related operational regions.
Offset between a local onboard clock and reference system time that appears as a range-equivalent measurement error term.
Rate of change of clock bias, often modeled as a state term that contributes to Doppler-like timing errors.
Operational navigation mode where direct GNSS support is unavailable or unreliable, requiring alternative sensing and estimation strategies.
Spatial relationship among observer, target, and reference assets that determines directional measurement sensitivity and observability.
Covariance-weighted measurement residual metric, typically expressed as e^T S^{-1} e, used to score consistency between predicted and observed measurements.
Assessment of how well available measurements constrain the navigation state, often quantified with matrix conditioning or singular-value diagnostics.
Positioning, Navigation, and Timing functions that together provide state estimation and time alignment for mission operations.
Geodesic angular mismatch between observation-derived and estimated frame orientation represented with unit quaternions.
Navigation approach using comparison of sensed surface features against terrain maps to estimate pose or position when external references are sparse.
Proposed confidence-scoring layer that evaluates residual, frame, timing, observability, and dynamics coherence alongside existing navigation estimators.
